member_findings.title: Handle rejected URMA switch submissions
member_findings.impact: After RecordUrmaResult has claimed the switch-in-progress gate, SubmitUrmaDataPlaneSwitch calls ThreadPool::Execute without handling its failure mode. The repository ThreadPool throws when the pool is already shut down, so a URMA failure observed during client shutdown or a late in-flight request can unwind out of the callback and terminate the process instead of marking the switch attempt failed. The tracker gate also remains claimed if submission does not complete normally, suppressing later failover decisions.
member_findings.fix: Submit the task through a non-throwing path or catch Execute exceptions, log the failure, and call FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt(false) on the reporting worker before returning false. Also avoid accepting new URMA failover callbacks once shutdown has started.

clusters.title: Multiple reviewers agree the raw conversation transcript should be removed

member_findings.title: Remove raw AI conversation transcript from the repository
member_findings.impact: The newly checked-in transcript exposes assistant hidden reasoning, model/tool metadata, source snippets, and command results to anyone with repository access. This normalizes committing raw agent logs, which can easily include credentials, private endpoints, or sensitive operational output in future runs.
member_findings.fix: Delete the .conversations transcript from the PR and keep raw agent logs outside version control. If a transcript artifact is required, add a redacted, purpose-built summary instead and ignore the raw .conversations directory.

member_findings.title: Remove generated conversation transcript from the repository
member_findings.impact: The added .conversations JSONL file is not source, test data, or durable documentation; it embeds an implementation chat transcript with stale intermediate code and tool output. Keeping it tracked will pollute repository search, confuse future maintainers about the authoritative design, and grow maintenance noise unrelated to the client failover feature.
member_findings.fix: Remove the .conversations/8c2f0b5d-3e4a-4b9c-8d2f-1b3c4d5e6f70.jsonl file from the PR, and add or verify an ignore rule for local/generated conversation transcripts if this directory can be produced by tooling.

clusters.title: Add negative tests for new URMA failover gflag validators

member_findings.title: Add negative tests for new URMA failover gflag validators
member_findings.impact: The PR adds validation that must reject non-finite or out-of-range success-rate ratios and a zero min-sample count, but the added UT/ST coverage only assigns valid values directly or writes valid config files. A regression that accepts `-urma_failover_success_rate_ratio=nan`, `1.1`, or `-urma_failover_min_sample_count=0` would leave an unsafe failover policy accepted by startup or hot config parsing without any test failing.
member_findings.fix: Add focused flag tests, for example in `tests/ut/common/flags/flags_test.cpp`, that call `SetCommandLineOption` or `ParseCommandLineFlags` for `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` values below 0, above 1, and non-finite inputs, plus `urma_failover_min_sample_count=0`, asserting rejection and preservation of the previous/default value. Keep the existing tracker/ST tests for valid dynamic-update behavior.

clusters.title: Avoid a second payload_info scan on URMA Get

member_findings.title: Avoid a second payload_info scan on URMA Get
member_findings.impact: Every successful remote URMA Get with payload metadata now calls HasUrmaTcpFallbackPayload, which linearly scans rsp.payload_info just before FillUrmaBuffer scans the same repeated field again. For batch Get/MGet with many small objects this adds avoidable protobuf traversal on a request-critical path even when no TCP fallback occurred.
member_findings.fix: Fold fallback detection into the existing FillUrmaBuffer payload_info loop, or have FillUrmaBuffer return whether any part_index entries were already present, then record the URMA result from that value without a separate pre-pass.

clusters.title: Align tracker comments with fixed-window behavior

member_findings.title: Align tracker comments with fixed-window behavior
member_findings.impact: The new API documents windowLengthMs as a sliding-window duration, but RecordUrmaResult actually settles a complete fixed window by resetting windowStartTimeMs_ and exchanging the counters. The PR description and gflag docs also describe complete-window evaluation, so this comment gives future maintainers the wrong mental model when tuning or modifying failover behavior.
member_findings.fix: Change the comment to describe a fixed/tumbling statistics window, for example: '@param[in] windowLengthMs Fixed statistics window duration in milliseconds; a later request settles the previous full window.'
